citations.description: Main test file — contains no idempotency test (no double-run of backfill in write mode)
Idempotency test exists here (heldout file), not in the required main test file

findings.criterion: re-running is idempotent — the test runs the script in WRITE mode (`--write`, NOT dry-run) twice: the first pass draws the caused_by link, and the second pass draws 0 new links because the bug already carries an active `caused_by` to its culprit (the script checks `_is_active_link` before writing).
findings.detail: The idempotency test (`test_write_is_idempotent`) is present only in `tests/scripts/test_backfill_caused_by_heldout.py` (line 94–110), NOT in `tests/scripts/test_backfill_caused_by.py` as the AC requires. The main test file contains only `test_proposes_single_culprit` and `test_end_to_end_exactly_one_link_from_two_bugs`; neither runs `backfill(write=True)` twice to verify the second call returns 0. The script's `_is_active_link` guard (line 66 of the script) is not exercised by any test in the required file.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
